轉會消息

已簽入：
Lautaro Martinez、Asamoah、De Vrij

將離隊：
Lisando Lopez、Rafinha、Cancelo
租借期至六月三十日，由於國米無於賽季結束前行使買斷權，三位球員將於七月返回母會。

Lopez有望加盟熱拿亞（Di Marzio）

Rafinha希望留喺國米，但國米目前財政不能負擔其轉會費用（38m），而巴塞拒絕減價及續租建議，雙方將於下星期再次會面。（Di Marzio）

Cancelo離國米越來越遠，狼隊出價4千萬已獲華西答應，經理人文迪斯積極撮合球員同狼隊。但Cancelo想踢大球會，祖雲達斯已向華西提出租借及4千萬強制買斷，雙方球會正在尋求共識，如果無結果，Cancelo將加盟狼隊。（Di Marzio）

傳聞離隊消息：
Nagatomo (轉會至加拉塔沙雷，對方出2m, 國米要4m）、Joao Mario (去向未明，但必定離隊）、Dalbert（轉至摩納哥，3m租金+24m買斷權）
（Di Marzio）

傳續約：
簡達華（續一年，至2021，薪金不變）（Sky）、米蘭達（續一年，至2020）（Di Marzio）

傳聞轉入：
Malcom
法甲波爾多21歲右翼，巴西人。波爾多要價5千萬歐元，國米出價4千萬。球員收到PSG、利記、多蒙特等球會邀請，但首選係國米，原因係相信去國米有助佢嘅發展。談判將會繼續進行，以收窄兩球會嘅差距。（Di Marzio）

Politano
國米曾嘗試向薩斯索羅提出報價，以8百萬歐元租金及1400萬買斷權，要求簽下Politano。報價未打動薩斯索羅，但國米會繼續觀察。（Di Marzio)

Nainggolan
奧西里奧曾向尼恩高蘭經理人查詢球員嘅情況，並且要求持續更新其轉會狀態。尼恩高蘭將會係國米中場首選目標，而球員身價不低於3千萬。（Di Marzio）

Barella
巴里拉經理人同為尼恩高蘭嘅經理人，史巴列堤亦都相當欣賞佢，但首選仍然係尼恩高蘭。巴里拉身價約為3500萬。（Di Marzio）

Dembele
熱刺中場丹比利合約期餘下一年，熱刺有意將佢出售以獲取餘剩價值，以免無償失去該名中場球員。國米被指對佢好有興趣，更傳出已報價1000萬歐元求購該名球員，當然係無下文，亦有傳出會將J. Mario加入為談判籌碼。（米蘭體育報）

Srna
薩克達36歲克羅地亞右翼，效力球會15季後，今季佢將會作出改變。昨日有傳佢會加盟卡利亞里，但同時呢次意大利之行亦引起國際米蘭注意。國米認為佢嘅經驗會好有幫助，但同時都好關注佢嘅體能狀態。國米將會一直留意該名球員，更加可能嘗試簽下佢。（Di Marzio)
鳩射巴 不如第1頁放埋ffp d野等人可以知下國米而家幾柒 要7月先開始買返人

自2011年國際足協推出財政公平原則（FFP），球會需要達至收支平衡，即係不能洗腳唔抹腳。

Uefa亦要求國米喺2018-19年之前遵守有關協議。咁所謂嘅收支平衡其實並非單純指轉會市場上嘅收支狀況，而係球會整體財政狀況。收入包括咗贊助與及其他營運收入，球會持有人直接投資亦在內。因此你會見到大球會依然可以大力支出，靠嘅就係球會收入，所以FFP真係好on9。

即使國際米蘭於贊助與及轉會上得到部分收入，國米仍然要喺2017-18財政年度結束之前籌措30m歐元。而國際米蘭喺2017-18年度嘅期限係去到本年6月30號，正因此國米唔能夠買斷簡些路及拉芬拿，避免再次增加支出。但點解蘇寧唔直接加大投資額，填補呢30m，暫時就不得而知，可能就係成日講嘅中國限制資金外流政策，令蘇寧要勒住褲頭。

所謂國米轉會窗會喺七月先開始，其實係因為七月份會展開新一年財政年度，即係2018-19年，無須顧忌上一年嘅收支問題。而新一年國米更加會得到歐冠名額額外獎金30m，幫助到國米轉會運作。

The agent of Milan Skriniar has revealed that five top clubs are after the Inter Milan defender.

The 23-year-old Skriniar recently joined Inter from Serie A side Sampdoria last summer and has impressed a lot this season. He appeared in 38 Serie A games, scoring four times. His performances have attracted interest from a host of clubs across Europe.

Skriniar's agent Karol Csonto recently revealed that the player has received offers from five top clubs, but he is not willing to move this summer. He told aktuality.sk: "Milan is very happy at Inter: the fans love it and he has played an amazing season, standing out as one of the best defenders ever.

"I can say that we are not in a position to request a transfer to the Nerazzurri club: there are offers of the best five clubs in the world, but we have no intention of putting pressure on Inter."

Csonto also revealed that they have an offer of an extension from Inter. He said: "There could be a proposal that makes Skriniar the highest paid defender in the world but, in any case, would only serve the certainty of playing him. And, in this sense, Inter has already put on the table a very attractive renewal proposal ".

Politano's agent: "Inter are very interested, we are working on it"

Inter's search for a winger moves on, the position is a priority for the nerazzuri directors on the transfer market. One name that the club has interest in is Malcom from Bordeaux: the Brazilian is in favor of a move, but his club has placed a high transfer value on the player. Another player who Inter are targeting is Matteo Politano. His agent, Davide Lippi, commented on the Sassuolo winger while at Inter's headquarters to finalize third choice goalkeeper Berni's renewal: "I was here for Berni. Politano to Inter? We are working on it. Are Inter interested? Very much so."

INTER TARGET £16M DEMBELE

Tottenham midfielder Mousa Dembele is a transfer target for Inter Milan, reports Gazzetta dello Sport.

However, the Serie A side face a struggle to land the Belgian through his agent Kia Joorabchian.

Inter believe they could land Dembele for around £16m, but Tottenham want nearly double that amount.

However, they may throw Joao Mario into a potential deal to land Dembele. Mario was on loan at West Ham last season.

Inter reach agreement with Argentinos Junior prospect Gonzalez

Inter have always had an affinity for Argentine talents and now they have set their sights on Nicolas Gonzalez. The Argentinos Junior talent born in ‘98 is commonly known as Nico, his goals and assists have impressed the nerazzurri who have already received the player’s approval for a potential transfer to Italy.

In fact about three weeks ago, Gonzalez and his agents were in Italy to address some bureaucratic matters concerning his passport which will soon be EU compliant, this trip was also an opportunity to reach an agreement with Inter on financial terms. The nerazzurri will now try to do the same with Argentinos Junior who are currently asking for €10 million, Inter could complete the deal in synergy with another Italian club like Sassuolo or Bologna, where the player could gain some experience in Serie A.

Inter will however face competition from Paris St Germain, who have expressed interest in the past day, but the Italian club is confident they have anticipated the competition by already having the player’s approval. Nicolas Gonzalez could be heading to Serie A next season

10m

Sabatini on Inter youth and Cancelo

Ex-Inter director Walter Sabatini believes they’ll “cash in” on youth team champions, but he’d be “annoyed” if Juventus got Joao Cancelo.

Sabatini resigned his post a couple of months ago in protest at Suning Group’s strategy, having been hired to co-ordinate both the San Siro club and Jiangsu Suning in the Chinese Super League.

Last night, the Nerazzurri beat Fiorentina 2-0 in extra time to take the Primavera youth team Scudetto for the second year running.

“The youth team really does represent the heritage of the business, of Inter and of Suning,” Sabatini told FCInter1908.it.

“I am very happy for the lads, especially as this success isn’t just for them, but produce revenue, because the players who win are always more appetising on the transfer market.

“Inter need to cash in, so my compliments to the lads and everyone at Inter who contributed to this success. I am no longer there, but I expect these players will be sold to raise funds and can be brought back with options and clauses.”

Sabatini negotiated to get Cancelo on loan from Valencia last summer with a €40m option to buy, but Inter refused to pay it and he now seems to be on his way to Juventus for a similar fee.

When asked about this, Sabatini’s response was – adjusted for a family site – this: “If that happens, I’ll get really annoyed.”

Roma may sell Nainggolan, the Belgian could be on his way away from the capital, and Inter could be his destination. There have been direct and indirect contacts made in an initial fashion, but not serious negotiations for Nainggolan's possible move to nerazzuro. Nonetheless, Roma are interested in hearing serious offers for him, and a move abroad could end up being a high possibility, given Roma's preference of selling him outside of Italy. Inter is an option for Nainggolan, and China remains a possibility too.

One youngster out and one youngster in. Inter is dealing with Atalanta, who closed a deal for Inter youngster Bettella. President Percassi has paid 7 million euros for the young defender. Inter will have a buyback clause and they have also found a deal to bring Bastoni to Inter soon than expected, as he was to stay another year at Atalanta. The Nerazzurri will soon decide whether to keep him or to send him out on loan with SPAL and Sassuolo interested.

'Malcom-Inter still on, Candreva…’

Agent Federico Pastorello has assured that talks are ‘continuing’ to take Malcom to Inter, while Antonio Candreva could make way for the Bordeaux winger.

Inter reportedly had a 40m bid for Malcom rejected on Monday, but Pastorello – who is working as an intermediary in the deal – believes the Brazilian could yet end up in Nerazzurro.

“It’s not over for Malcom, we’re continuing to work,” he told Calciomercato.com.

As for his client Candreva, Pastorello added: “He’s happy to stay in Milan, but we’ll see if something comes out. We’ll evaluate if it does.”

replies.msg: 幾時先有得回家

Roma have been released from their FFP settlement agreement, while Inter remain and an investigation into Paris Saint-Germain has been closed.

UEFA released a statement this evening on the Financial Fair Play status of several clubs.

“The CFCB Investigatory Chamber confirmed that FC Krasnodar, FC Lokomotiv Moscow, AS Monaco FC, AS Roma and FC Zenit St Petersburg have been considered in compliance with the requirements and overall objective of their agreements. Consequently, they have all exited the settlement regime.”

This means the Giallorossi no longer have to sell players to balance the books or face specific limitations or fines.

On the other hand, “Fenerbahçe SK, FC Internazionale Milano and Trabzonspor AŞ have partially fulfilled the targets set for season 2017/18.

“As a consequence, the foreseen conditional sporting measures, such as the transfer restrictions and the limitation on the number of players in the List A, will not be lifted and will continue to apply in the season 2018/19. Additional financial contributions, if applicable, will be withheld as specified in each respective agreement.

“The settlement regime will continue to apply for season 2018/19 for both FC Internazionale Milano and Trabzonspor AŞ; and up to and including season 2019/20 for Fenerbahçe SK; as originally foreseen.”

This means Inter will have to evaluate the expenses on the transfer market and balance the books even for the last two years, when they were not in Europe.

Despite some rather controversial moves, Paris Saint-Germain will escape sanction for their FFP status.

“The CFCB Investigatory Chamber further decided to close the investigation into Paris Saint-Germain. Such decision follows a detailed review of transfer contracts and an analysis of the related management accounts which confirmed that such transactions were in line with the UEFA Club Licensing & Financial Fair Play Regulations.

“Furthermore, the chamber concluded that after significant fair value adjustments of several club sponsorship contracts - on the basis of evaluations performed by independent third party assessors - the break-even result of the club remains within acceptable deviation for the financial years ending in 2015, 2016 and 2017.

“The financial impact of transfer activities as from the 2017 summer – up to and including the upcoming transfer window - and compliance with the break-even requirement for the 2018 financial year will remain under close scrutiny and will be thoroughly looked at in the coming weeks.”

Nonetheless, PSG will effectively have to sell players for €60m by the end of the month to continue the break-even requirement.

Galatasaray and Maccabi Tel Aviv were not in compliance with the break-even requirement and have agreed new settlement agreements.

Olympique Marseille were fined €100,000.
FFP公佈左 國米下季繼續一路柒住FFP

Inter's transfer market is currently in a tough situation due to the Financial Fair Play requirement that Inter forces them to make a 30 million euro profit by June 30. The Nerazzurri are thinking about Moussa Dembele for Luciano Spalletti's squad and the club are planning a strategy to the Belgian to Italy.

It would be to keep Dembele at Inter for one year and let him leave for Jiangsu Suning for the following season. At this moment, even though there are no concrete offers from China, the player's entourage could be to gain as much as possible from this hypothetical transfer. Inter have studied this plan and to gain an important reinforcement for next season, they are trying to convince the player with an important contract at Jiangsu Suning for the next years. Inter are trying to put into place a winning strategy and their objective is Dembele for the midfield.

Agent of reported Arsenal target hints at Inter Milan exit

Marcelo Brozovic's agent opens the door for the Arsenal target to leave Inter Milan this summer
Marcelo Brozovic could leave Inter Milan in the summer transfer window amid interest from several clubs, the Arsenal target’s agent has claimed.

The Gunners are being linked with a move to sign the Croatia international this summer to bolster Unai Emery’s options in the middle of the park.

Brozovic has been a key member of Inter Milan’s team since his move to the San Siro outfit from Croatian side Dinamo Zagreb in 2015.

The 25-year-old scored four goals and made nine assists in 24 starts and 10 substitute appearances in the current campaign.

The Croatia midfielder is thought to have a €50m contract release clause, although Arsenal could struggle to match that proposed fee.

Brozovic’s agent has opened the door for the Inter star to leave Milan this summer amid talk of interest from the Gunners.

“Yes, I can confirm that several great clubs follow Marcelo and they are informed, but I can not name them,” his agent is quoted as saying by Calciomercato.

“Marcelo considers Inter as a home of his own. He likes Milan and is preparing to play the Champions League, but whether or not he will remain dependent on Inter and the market.”

Arsenal signed Switzerland captain Stephan Lichtsteiner in a free transfer from Serie A giants Juventus earlier this month.

The Gunners finished in sixth spot in the Premier League table last season to miss out on a spot in the Champions League.

The north London side missed out on silverware in Arsene Wenger’s final season after a 3-0 loss to Manchester City in the League Cup final.

Barca's Vidal wanted by Inter

Inter are reportedly pursuing a deal for out-of-favour Barcelona defender Aleix Vidal following their failure to capture Joao Cancelo from Valencia.

The Milanese giants failed to activate their €35m option to sign Cancelo, who spent last season on loan at the club, which expired at the end of May.

A report originating in Gazzetta dello Sport now explains the Nerazzurri have switched their attention to more affordable players, which has taken them to Vidal.

The 28-year-old is surplus to requirements at Barca, having appeared for more than 45 minutes just six times last season and an ‘affordable’ deal is probable, says the reports.

Despite long-term ankle issues, there are said to be no concerns over this scuppering a deal while his style of play is said to replicate the fit of Cancelo.

Inter are said to be hopeful of agreeing a season-long loan for the Spaniard with an option to buy for €10m, although Barca would be holding out for €15m.

Vidal has also been linked with a return to former club Sevilla, whom he left for Barca in 2015, for a number of months.

Regarding Nainggolan, there have been new contacts between the two clubs but Roma would rather have a bigger fee than players added to the deal. They'll also keep working on that front.

Kim Jong Un is a passionate Inter supporter and politician Antonio Razzi avoided discussing Juventus in the interests of “world peace.”
The bizarre revelation came from former Senator Razzi’s interview with Tuttosport, in which he gave anecdotes from his time in Silvio Berlusconi’s Italian government.
“It all started in 2011, the year Kim took over from his father Kim Yong Il as Supreme Leader of North Korea. You know that as a young man he had studied in Bern and would go to San Siro to watch Inter and Milan?

“He really likes Italian football and appreciates our technical capabilities. He supports Inter. I am very much a Juve fan. We never talked about it because there is too much rivalry between Inter and Juventus, so I didn’t want to create any reason for attrition. There was world peace at stake here, after all!
“I thought to myself: ‘Anto, don’t talk about Juve to an Interista, otherwise he’ll shoot you!’”

金仔都係 Interista

Inter and Napoli eye Di Maria

Angel Di Maria could leave Paris Saint-Germain this summer, with Napoli and Inter both eager for his signature.

The Argentina international does not seem ready to sign a new deal and his current contract in Paris expires in June 2019.

With this in mind, a sale would appear to be inevitable and Serie A is calling.

Inter have long been linked with Di Maria and Mediaset Premium claim director Piero Ausilio pledged to go for him if they qualified for the Champions League.

On the other hand, Carlo Ancelotti’s arrival at Napoli gives the Partenopei a precious card to play in this gamble for the 30-year-old wide forward.

The tactician already worked with Di Maria at Real Madrid in 2013-14.

https://www.football-italia.net/123071/inter-and-napoli-eye-di-maria

Inter Milan star Milan Skriniar has revealed that he is happy the club has rejected an offer from Manchester United for him and he is happy to stay at the San Siro this summer.

Skriniar has become one of the Serie A's best defenders since his arrival from Sampdoria last summer. His performances have helped him capture the attention of the two Manchester clubs. Skriniar appeared in every single Serie A game last season, scoring four times too.

In an interview that Skriniar gave to the Slovakian Football Website, he confirmed that he is happy that the nerazzurri have rejected an offer from United and he is happy to stay at the club.

He said: "I am happy and proud to have been linked with Manchester United, but I'm even happier that Inter has decided to refuse their offer and I am very happy to stay here. "

Radja Nainggolan admits ‘leaving Roma wasn’t my idea’ but assures ‘Inter wanted me more than anyone else and I can only thank them.’

Nainggolan is set to join Inter in a €24m deal that includes Primavera starlet Nicolo Zaniolo and full-back Davide Santon, and the Belgian midfielder expressed his delight at reuniting with Luciano Spalletti, even if ‘it was Roma who wanted to sell me’.

“I’ll always carry the Giallorossi colours and fans in my heart, but it was the club who wanted to sell me,” he told Gazzetta dello Sport.

“I’ll always carry every moment I’ve lived through with these colours in my heart. I leave Roma after giving everything in these wonderful seasons.

“Inter really wanted me, more than anyone else. I can only thank them. I’ll find a great Coach in Spalletti and I’m happy to work with him again.

“I want to thank every fan for the seasons we had together. They always supported me and showed great affection. I’ll never forget it.

“Leaving wasn’t my idea, it was a decision made by the club but one that I respected.”

Radja Nainggolan has spent the past day Montecarlo, arriving on Thursday evening to discuss, along with his agent Alessandro Beltrami, the details of the contract offer from Inter. The Belgian has requested several changes to the deal as proposed, mainly related to his image rights. The contract could be slated to last a year longer than Nainggolan's current deal at Roma, and would expire in 2022 or 2021 with the option for another year. Over the weekend the parties will continue to work on finalizing the deal: the medicals for Nainggolan with Inter could be undertaken at the start of next week (starting Monday he will be in Milan), between Tuesday and Wednesday.

除定褲

Inter continues to be very active on the transfer market and for the midfield their first choice for a new signing is Dembele, but they have sent out a feeler for William Carvalho. Born in 1992, he amassed 24 appearances, 1 goal and 2 assists in the Portuguese league with Sporting CP, a club of which many players became free agents, due to the incidents that happened with the fans.

In theory, Carvalho could arrive at Inter as a free agent. The situation is not so linear, however, as Sporting is threatening to dispute FIFA's decision to let the players break their contracts. Therefore, whoever signs Carvalho (and the other free agents) could face eventual economic sanctions. Inter is evaluating the situation of signing the player and the possible future implications.

Fredy Guarin “would be prepared to cut his salary for a return to Inter” from Shanghai Shenhua, said his agent.

The Colombia international midfielder was sold to the Chinese Super League club for €13m in January 2016.

“He renewed his contract for another two years, but not everything necessarily goes the way you expect. We’ll see what Inter will say and do,” agent Marcelo Ferreyra told Passione Inter.

“Fredy has kept himself in great shape, both physically and mentally. He is still highly-motivated and faced with a call from Inter, he would be ready.

“His wages aren’t a problem, as he would be prepared to cut his salary for a return to Inter.”

Guarin wore the Inter jersey from 2012 to 2016 and he turns 32 next week.

Agent Federico Pastorello assures Antonio Candreva will extend his Inter contract ‘soon’ and that talks have taken place for Malcom.

Candreva has been linked with moves away from Inter, but Pastorello reiterated the winger was staying put, while revealing a deal for Malcom could happen once the Nerazzurri meet their FFP obligations, given his role as intermediary in negotiations for the Bordeaux forward.

“Malcom to Inter? I’d be happy to confirm this deal, he’s a player whom Inter like,” he told Domenica Sportiva.

“I’ve talked to Ausilio, but I think that for now Inter are more focused on securing the capital gains that are needed by UEFA and only after that do I think they’ll pursue new faces.

“Candreva? I don’t think his destiny can change, he wants to stay at Inter. His contract? We’re at the details stage. It’ll be announced soon, until 2021.”


斬華續約，Malcom soon

FC Internazionale Milano is pleased to announce the appointment of Luca Danovaro as the club’s new Chief Marketing Officer. Danovaro will join Inter at the beginning of July and will be responsible for brand development strategies as well as operations spanning fan engagement, CRM, business and digital marketing, event organisation, Inter Forever and the production of video and digital content. His aim will be to continue to build on the progress made last season, which saw the club record several significant achievements. 

“I feel honoured and excited to have been given the opportunity to embrace this new challenge,” said Danovaro. “I’m looking forward to working with the team and using my experience to serve the club, with the aim of continuing to enhance the Inter brand around the world by using innovation and technology to enrich the fan experience. I would like to thank the club for showing faith in me and giving me this opportunity.”

“On behalf of everyone at the club I would like to welcome Luca to Inter,” said Nerazzurri CEO Alessandro Antonello. “I have no doubt that his professionalism and proven managerial expertise will be of huge benefit to our club. Luca will help to forge an even closer bond with the millions of Nerazzurri fans that follow the team from all over the globe. Together, we will continue to strive to take Inter to the top of world football.”

During his career, Danovaro has worked with top brands such as Moleskine, Samsung and Nike, acquiring knowledge and experience that will enable him to make an important contribution to the club.

Inter Milan confirm agreement with Roma to sign Radja Nainggolan

Inter Milan have confirmed that an agreement has been reached with Italian rivals Roma to sign midfielder Radja Nainggolan.

The 30-year-old, who last month announced his international retirement after missing out on a place in Belgium's World Cup squad, passed a medical with the Nerazzurri and agreed personal terms on Monday.

Nainggolan scored six goals and registered the same number of assists for Roma last season, having seen a move to Chelsea fall through in the summer.

Inter are close to officially signing the ex-Piacenza and Cagliari ace, however, with a further announcement planned before the end of Tuesday.

"The conditions have been met for a deal for Radja Nainggolan," Inter's sporting director Piero Ausilio told the club's website. "He is a top player. Everything will be made official [on Tuesday] and then we will say something more."

Nainggolan has previously worked under Inter manager Luciano Spalletti at Roma.

Rafinha 'ready for anything'

Rafinha Alcantara has explained he is ‘ready for anything’ but ‘right now I’m a Barcelona player’ after returning from a loan spell at Inter.

The Brazilian joined the Milanese giants on a loan deal from the Catalan giants with a €35m option to buy attached but despite negotiations, no deal was reached.

“Inter was a spectacular experience,” Rafinha explained to Lancenet.

“It was a style of football and a language I didn’t know. It was important for me to get back to playing as many games as possible after my injury. I knew my ability to adapt quickly would be important.

“Thanks to my teammates and the Nerazzurri fans, I was very well received and I regained my confidence in playing my style of football.

“I had a great time at Inter and I was thinking of staying. I was really convinced that with qualification for the Champions League I’d have stayed, but it didn’t happen.

“Football is still a business and sometimes people’s wishes aren’t fulfilled. Right now I’m a Barcelona player.

“I still haven’t met with the club, but in my head I have the idea of presenting myself for pre-season training on July 11 and then we’ll see.

“I’m ready for anything.”

Speaking to Inter TV about his recent transfer, here are Radja Nainggolan’s first few words as an Inter player: “I did not expect such a warm welcome. I am happy. It will now be down to me to earn yet more applause.

“Affection from the fans is important for any player and it means that they’re enthusiastic, just like I am. I hope that we can now enjoy more happy times together. Spalletti? My best season was with him and I now hope that I can do just as well. I am at the Coach’s disposal and I am pleased to be back working with him. Why I chose Inter? Confidence is key and I received calls from Director Ausilio, the Coach and Zanetti. They all made me feel important and my decision was simple. I am ready to go ahead of this new adventure.

"A message for the fans? I still don’t know them too well but seeing how they welcomed me here, I hope that they can continue to support the squad in the same manner. We can do well in the league and also in the Champions League. Inter have an important history behind them and they must now return to playing their part in Europe.”

史巴列堤要多兩個攻擊球員，分別係普列坦奴同馬爾甘。

國米喺過去數小時與薩斯索羅會面，加速普列坦奴轉會，並達成協議：外借連25M買斷。而國米青年軍Zappa 同 Merola將會轉投至薩斯索羅。未來數小時國米將會同普列坦奴經理人傾埋啲細節。

而馬爾甘仍然有機會轉會至國米，但佢嚟到將會意味住簡達華或者卡拉莫會離隊。

普列坦奴會踢後備，好在佢連左翼都踢到，而目前Perisic係無後備，除非計埋艾達條辣殺
而技術特點上，普列坦奴唔係賣腳下功夫同花巧盤扭技術，反而係衝刺力、破門能力同決斷，呢一點同護球、技術好嘅Verdi都唔同。
至於呢幾項特點係對打反擊好有幫助，記得佢兩回合對我打都同Berardi創造到好多次反擊，快到無人回到防，好似入左無人之境咁打到我到個中後場死死下。反觀返國米，前面三個都唔係快放衝刺型嘅球員，講埋卡拉莫佢比較熱愛盤扭，而且經驗尚淺，外借對佢發展有利。

普列坦奴會踢後備，好在佢連左翼都踢到，而目前Perisic係無後備，除非計埋艾達條辣殺
而技術特點上，普列坦奴唔係賣腳下功夫同花巧盤扭技術，反而係衝刺力、破門能力同決斷，呢一點同護球、技術好嘅Verdi都唔同。
至於呢幾項特點係對打反擊好有幫助，記得佢兩回合對我打都同Berardi創造到好多次反擊，快到無人回到防，好似入左無人之境咁打到我到個中後場死死下。反觀返國米，前面三個都唔係快放衝刺型嘅球員，講埋卡拉莫佢比較熱愛盤扭，而且經驗尚淺，外借對佢發展有利。

Inter's transfer market remains to be far from over. After the purchases of Stefan De Vrij, Kwadwo Asamoah, Federico Dimarco, Lautaro Martínez and most recently Radja Nainggolan, the Nerazzurri club has asked Atletico Madrid about Croatian full-back Sime Vrsaljko.

Today, the Nerazzurri met for lunch with the sporting director of the Spanish side in which they officially asked about the former Sassuolo defender on loan, with the right of redemption. However, Atletico Madrid has no intention of surrendering the player to these conditions, so the parties will continue to negotiate in the coming days.

Nainggolan: ‘Can’t wait for Inter’

Radja Nainggolan “can’t wait to start this adventure” after sealing his move to Inter from Roma.

The midfielder has joined the Nerazzurri in a €38m deal, paying €24m cash and sending Nicolò Zaniolo and Davide Santon to the capital club.

“I’m happy to be here, I can’t wait to start this adventure,” Nainggolan said at his presentation Press conference.

Inter wanted me very much. I hope to win something, also for personal reasons because so far I haven’t managed to do that.

“I find a Coach here [Luciano Spalletti] who I did very well with before. We can get results, and it’s possible to win something as soon as possible.

“Did Spalletti convince me to come? It wasn’t just that, but it’s certainly good to see him again. I already know the way he works.

“The Coach will decide what I give to the team tactically, he’s smart enough to know.”

Edin Dzeko and Mauro Icardi have similar characteristics, can Nainggolan form a similar relationship with the Beneamata captain?

“For me they’re two great strikers, with different characteristics. Icardi is more about scoring, whereas Dzeko has a different makeup and works more for the team.

“I got on really well with Edin, and I hope it’ll be the same with Mauro.

“I’ve already spoken to him, I told him I was fired-up and he told me he was equally fired-up.

“Who am I looking forward to playing with? Mauro goes without saying, and there are a lot of quality players here.

“When [Ivan] Perisic is on form he can make the difference, and there are many others. I’ll do my best to help them achieve results.”

The Ninja rejected moves away from Roma in the past, and was asked what went wrong between him and the club.

“I don’t want to talk about it too much, I want to look at the present,” Nainggolan said.

“It could have ended differently but I don’t want to say anything more. During the negotiations I called everyone, and it’s very important to feel trusted.

“I hope to repay Inter on the pitch.”

Nainggolan will wear the number 14 shirt, as his usual number 4 has been retired in tribute to Javier Zanetti.

Martinez: ‘Follow Icardi and Zanetti’

New Inter signing Lautaro Martinez wants to follow in the footsteps of his compatriots Diego Milito and Javier Zanetti.

The striker officially completed his move from Racing Club earlier today, and spoke to the Nerazzurri’s official website about his new club.

“I’m very happy, we were working toward this and now everything is done,” said Martinez.

“I’m very happy for the stage of my career that I’ll experience here. Of course I followed last season, but first of all I want to settle down, meet my teammates and the Coach [Luciano Spalletti].

“After that I want to understand what I’ll be doing on the pitch, and of course I’ll give everything for this shirt.

“I expect to adapt quickly, but obviously for me it will be a completely different style of play to the Argentinian one.

“I think Serie A will be crucial for me to learn more and add new qualities to my game.

“I was lucky enough to share a dressing room with Diego Milito at Racing Club, so he told me what Inter means many times.

“I also spoke with Javier Zanetti, they left an important mark on this club and I want to follow in their footsteps.”
